title: Git Branching
description: Learn to create, list, switch, rename, and delete branches using modern Git branch commands.

tasks.hint: Use git branch with no arguments.
tasks.explanation: git branch lists local branch names with the current branch highlighted.

tasks.prompt: Create a new branch named 'feature-auth' without switching to it.
tasks.hint: Use git branch followed by the branch name.
tasks.explanation: git branch <name> creates a branch pointer at the current commit.

tasks.hint: Use git switch develop or git checkout develop.
tasks.explanation: git switch updates HEAD to point at the target branch.

tasks.prompt: Create and switch to a new branch named 'hotfix'.
tasks.hint: Use git switch -c hotfix or git checkout -b hotfix.
tasks.explanation: The -c/-b flag creates a branch and checks it out in one step.

tasks.prompt: List local branches with their latest commit hash and message.
tasks.explanation: git branch -v shows the tip commit for each local branch.
git branch -m renames the current branch.
git branch -d safely deletes a branch that has been merged.

tasks.prompt: List all local and remote-tracking branches.
tasks.explanation: git branch -a includes remote branches like origin/main.
